title: varronia suffruticosa
intro: Varronia suffruticosa, also known as the bush varonia or bush tourton, is a tropical tree native to the Americas, particularly in the Caribbean and Central and South America. It has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of Varronia suffruticosa include teas, infusions, and ointments, which can be made by boiling the leaves or roots in water or mixing them with other herbs to create a remedy.
